report_name: Microsoft Teams Relay Servers Abused in DragonForce Ransomware Attack

threat_actors.name: DragonForce
threat_actors.description: A sophisticated, cartel-like ransomware group that deployed the Backdoor.Turn malware to abuse Microsoft Teams infrastructure for C&C. They have shown increasing technical maturity, moving to custom tooling and advanced techniques like BYOVD attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Gained initial access to the victim's network by likely exploiting an unknown vulnerability in a public-facing SQL or MSSQL server.

attack_patterns.name: DLL Side-Loading
attack_patterns.description: Used a DLL sideloading attack, initiated from a ZIP archive disguised as a hotfix, to execute code and download additional malware.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
attack_patterns.description: Employed a Bring-Your-Own-Vulnerable-Driver (BYOVD) strategy, exploiting flaws in multiple signed drivers (HWAuidoOs2Ec.sys, wsftprm.sys, etc.) to gain kernel-level access.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: Used a BYOVD attack to gain kernel-level access, which allowed them to terminate security processes on compromised systems.

attack_patterns.name: Process Injection
attack_patterns.description: Injected the Backdoor.Turn malware into the legitimate DbgView64.exe process to maintain stealthy, long-term access.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Web Browsers
attack_patterns.description: The Backdoor.Turn malware has the capability to exfiltrate credentials from installed web browsers.

attack_patterns.name: Domain Trust Discovery
attack_patterns.description: Conducted network reconnaissance, including LDAP/AD mapping, to understand the victim's domain environment.

attack_patterns.name: Protocol Tunneling
attack_patterns.description: The Backdoor.Turn malware tunnels its C&C traffic through legitimate Microsoft TURN servers to evade detection.

attack_patterns.name: Proxy
attack_patterns.description: Abused Microsoft Teams TURN relay servers as a proxy to camouflage C&C communications as legitimate Teams activity.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Deployed DragonForce ransomware to encrypt files across the victim's environment after mapping the network and exfiltrating data.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Exfiltrated sensitive data from the victim network before deploying the ransomware payload.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: Established a C&C channel over a QUIC session, a web protocol, which was tunneled through Microsoft's TURN servers.

malware.name: Backdoor.Turn
malware.description: A custom Go-based backdoor that abuses Microsoft Teams TURN relay servers to tunnel C&C traffic over QUIC. It was injected into DbgView64.exe for post-exploitation persistence and has RAT capabilities.

malware.name: DragonForce ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload deployed by the DragonForce group to encrypt files after data exfiltration.

malware.name: ABYSSWORKER
malware.description: A custom-built malicious driver used by DragonForce for defense evasion in their BYOVD attack. It has also been observed in Medusa ransomware attacks.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Used to drop a ZIP archive disguised as a tech support hotfix, which initiated a DLL sideloading attack.

tools.name: DbgView64.exe
tools.description: A legitimate Sysinternals diagnostic tool that was abused by having the Backdoor.Turn malware injected into its process to evade detection.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2023-52271
vulnerabilities.description: Vulnerability in the wsftprm.sys driver, exploited as part of a BYOVD attack to gain kernel-level access and disable security tools.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2025-61155
vulnerabilities.description: Vulnerability in the GameDriverX64.sys driver, exploited as part of a BYOVD attack to gain kernel-level access and disable security tools.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2025-1055
vulnerabilities.description: Vulnerability in the K7RKScan.sys driver, exploited as part of a BYOVD attack to gain kernel-level access and disable security tools.

identities.name: U.S. services firm
identities.description: A U.S.-based services firm targeted by the DragonForce ransomware group, who dwelled in the network for one to two months before deploying ransomware.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: Vendor whose Microsoft Teams TURN relay servers and Skype identity services were abused by the Backdoor.Turn malware for command and control.

infrastructure.name: Microsoft Teams TURN Relay Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Legitimate Microsoft Teams TURN relay servers abused by the Backdoor.Turn malware to tunnel C&C traffic, camouflaging it as standard Teams activity.

indicators.name: DragonForce BYOVD Vulnerable Driver Load
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: DragonForce BYOVD Vulnerable Driver Load
id: 2f8b1c9a-4e2d-4f8a-b1c9-a2d4e8f1c9a2
status: experimental
description: Detects the loading of known vulnerable drivers used by DragonForce in BYOVD attacks to disable security tools.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/microsoft-teams-relay-servers-abused-in-dragonforce-ransomware-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-17
tags:
  - attack.t1068
  - attack.privilege_escalation
level: high
logsource:
  category: driver_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ImageLoaded|endswith:
      - '\HWAuidoOs2Ec.sys'
      - '\wsftprm.sys'
      - '\GameDriverX64.sys'
      - '\K7RKScan.sys'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the loading of known vulnerable drivers used in the DragonForce BYOVD attack.

indicators.name: DragonForce Malicious Tool Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: DragonForce Malicious Tool Execution
id: 3a9c2d0b-5f3e-4g9b-c2d0-b3e5f9c2d0b3
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution or loading of custom malicious tools used by DragonForce, including the ABYSSWORKER driver and Backdoor.Turn.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/microsoft-teams-relay-servers-abused-in-dragonforce-ransomware-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-17
tags:
  - attack.t1055
  - attack.t1562.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\ABYSSWORKER'
      - '\Backdoor.Turn'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of custom DragonForce malware like ABYSSWORKER driver or Backdoor.Turn.

indicators.name: Anomalous TURN/QUIC Traffic from Server
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous TURN or QUIC Traffic from Server
id: 4b0d3e1c-6g4f-4h0c-d3e1-c4f6g0d3e1c4
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects anomalous TURN (UDP 3478-3481) or QUIC (UDP 443) traffic originating from servers or non-user workstations.
  This may indicate abuse of services like Microsoft Teams for C2, as seen with Backdoor.Turn.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/microsoft-teams-relay-servers-abused-in-dragonforce-ransomware-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-17
tags:
  - attack.t1090
  - attack.t1572
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_turn:
    Protocol: 'udp'
    DestinationPort|between: '3478-3481'
  selection_quic:
    Protocol: 'udp'
    DestinationPort: 443
  filter_workstation:
    Image|startswith: 'C:\Users\'
  condition: (selection_turn or selection_quic) and not filter_workstation
indicators.description: Detects anomalous TURN/QUIC traffic from servers, which could indicate abuse of Microsoft Teams for C2 communications by malware like Backdoor.Turn.

indicators.name: Process Injection into DbgView64.exe
indicators.pattern:
title: Process Injection into DbgView64.exe
id: 5c1e4f2d-7h5g-4i1d-e4f2-d5g7h1e4f2d5
status: experimental
description: Detects process injection into the legitimate Sysinternals tool DbgView64.exe, a technique used by DragonForce to hide their Backdoor.Turn malware.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/microsoft-teams-relay-servers-abused-in-dragonforce-ransomware-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-17
tags:
  - attack.t1055
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_access
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetImage|endswith: '\DbgView64.exe'
  filter_legit:
    SourceImage|endswith: 
      - '\procexp64.exe'
      - '\procmon.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects process injection into the legitimate DbgView64.exe tool, a technique used by DragonForce to hide the Backdoor.Turn malware.
